title.rendered: How Does Pasteurization Influence Paneer Quality? Read Along To Know

content.rendered:
Pasteurization affects the quality of paneer by enhancing the texture and the body, enhancing the flavour, improving the yield and solid recovery and extending the shelf life of paneer. Paneer is a type of cheese that is non-melting and non-ageing. It is produced by curdling milk with a food acid like vinegar and lemon juice. Pasteurization is an integral process that paneer goes through to ensure that harmful microorganisms are killed. Apart from eliminating pathogens, pasteurization affects the quality of paneer, which is elaborated in this blog. 

Enhanced texture and body

Pasteurization affects the texture and the body of paneer quality by altering the chemical and physical structure of milk through denaturing whey proteins. The right pasteurization, around 80℃ to 90℃, causes a coprecipitation of whey proteins and casein, forming a firm and spongy texture that maintains its texture during cooking and can be sliced. The heat helps to create the firm texture, whilst overheating it can make the paneer too dry or rubbery. 

Safety and extended shelf life 

Pasteurization functions as a barrier against pathogens, and hence it determines the shelf life of paneer. Unprocessed, raw milk can carry various bacteria that shorten the shelf life and can be harmful for consumption. The heating process of pasteurization kills the pathogens, hence making it safe for consumption. This process also kills microorganisms that shorten the shelf life of paneer.

Flavour profile 

The end result of paneer has a milder flavour. The pasteurization process inactivates the raw milk enzymes, which prevent the development of rancidity, resulting in a cleaner and milder flavour. Unpasteurized milk has a wild flavour because of the enzymes present in the raw milk. The heating treatment also gives paneer a clean white look, resulting in a standardized taste and colour. 

Improved yield and solid recovery 

Through pasteurization, high yield and solid recovery are achieved. This is done through the denaturisation that takes place during the heating treatment. When the protein binds with the casein during naturisation, the whey proteins are trapped in the curd during acidification, hence improving solid recovery. The heating process results in a dense and complex protein network, which improves the weight or the yield of paneer. 

Machines used to achieve artisan paneer quality

Paneer pasteurizing system: The system ensures that the paneer remains pasteurized to prevent bacterial formation. 

Paneer press machine: The machine forms the paneer into shape.

Milk coagulation VAT equipment: This machine converts milk from the initial state to a semi-solid state. 

Paneer cooling system: The system keeps paneer in a cool state to maintain its quality and to avoid spoilage. 

Conclusion 

The right pasteurization process ensures that the paneer is safe from pathogens, it has high quality and a standard flavour. Manufacturers should invest in the right manufacturing machines to achieve a standard quality and for scalable production. To get these premium machines, producers can get in touch with NK Dairy Equipments. Mrs. Himpreet Kaur and her team supply and install paneer pasteurizing systems and other dairy systems. Our team ensures to provide guidance for clients to get the required machines for their business. 

FAQ’s 

  1. Does pasteurization result in loss of nutrient value in paneer?

Pasteurization does not decrease the overall nutrient value, but rather the process kills pathogens. 

  1. Does pasteurization affect the yield of paneer?

Pasteurization improves the yield of paneer by triggering the denaturation of whey proteins, which causes them to bind with casein and also results in a solid recovery.

title.rendered: How Do Modern Cream Separators Boost The Profitability For Ghee Producers?

content.rendered:
Modern cream separators rapidly boost profitability for ghee producers by maximising the fat content in the cream, which is achieved through the centrifugal force generated by the machine. It captures up to ninety-eight per cent of the fat compared to the traditional methods, which directly increases the yield of high-value cream from the same volume of milk. These machines are easily available from any popular dairy equipment manufacturer in India. This machine allows for precise standardisation of cream richness, which should contain forty to sixty per cent fat, resulting in consistent and premium quality ghee in every batch. This improvement in the quality and better production can increase the overall revenue of the small as well as large-scale industries. Moreover, efficient separation reduces fat loss and accelerates a faster rate of interest. Let’s discuss in detail the major ways modern cream separators can boost profitability for ghee producers in this blog.

Key profitability drivers

  • Maximised recovery and fat recovery: The modern separation processes the cream at high centrifugal force with continuous rotation of six thousand to ten thousand RPM. This process reduces the fat losses up to 0.05 per cent in skim milk. This process results in more cream and, therefore, more production of ghee from the same quantity of milk as compared to the traditional methods.
  • Reduced operational costs: The automated system minimises the need for manual labour which automatically reduces the operational costs. The efficient and consistent separation of the cream can reduce the energy consumption for heating by fifteen to thirty per cent.
  • Consistent quality and higher value: The milk contains the standard fat content of forty to sixty per cent cream when processed with modern cream separators. This ensures consistent quality and quantity of ghee produced from every batch. This indirectly increases customer loyalty and allows for premium pricing.
  • Faster processing: Modern separators can handle hundreds and thousands of litres per hour. It increases the processing speed, which saves time and reduces the need for labour. This fast processing yields drastically higher production compared to the traditional methods.
  • Valuable byproducts: This single process is used for producing ghee, and it is also suitable for producing high-quality skimmed milk that can be sold or used for other products. This approach ensures that each component of the milk is properly used which reduces the wastage and maximises the output.

Conclusion

Modern cream separators have become extremely necessary for the profitability of commercial ghee production. This process maximises fat recovery, reduces labour-intensive tasks and ensures consistent product quality throughout the automation. They allow the producers to increase the amount of material passing through the machine, which successfully achieves thirty per cent more output from the same quantity of raw milk. In spite of the fact that this set-up requires a high initial investment, installing cream separators can increase the efficiency of the cream production by maintaining ninety-six to ninety-eight per cent fat in cream, which ensures a rapid return on investment. By purchasing a cream separator from NK dairy equipment, we guarantee to provide quick installations of the machinery, either for small-scale or large-scale industry, under the guidance of our expert Mrs. Himpreet Kaur. This equipment will act as an important part of the profitability and your scalable dairy business. 

FAQs

  1. What is the ideal cream fat percentage for ghee production?

It is generally recommended to set the cream separator to a fat content between forty per cent and sixty per cent for the best results. 

  1. Can a cream separator work on homogenised milk?

No, the homogenisation process reduces the fat globules to a size that makes it impossible for a standard cream separator to separate the cream from the skimmed milk. 

  1. How often should I clean the cream separator?

The machinery should be disassembled and cleaned properly after use to prevent bacterial contamination and to maintain hygiene by complying with the safety standards for food. Always use food equipment-specific detergent to clean the equipment.

title.rendered: Are You Looking To Know About The Indian Dairy Ecosystem? Read On To Know About It In Briefly

content.rendered:
Due to the increase in tension between India and the US,  the trade between them also suffered a threat. Trump wanted to impose huge taxes on the dairy products along with a huge market share. India is one of the largest milk producing countries that produces more than 180 million tonnes in a year. The dairy market is a great contributor in the rural areas as many people farm both cows and buffaloes to produce milk. The people of India also consume a large part of its own milk consumption. The dairy also has less imports due to high tariffs. The dairy market is largely ruled by marginal landlords and small landless labourers. The national dairy development board is the foundation for milk collection, processing and marketing. The National Digital Livestock Mission has created a digital database to track animal breeding, health and vaccination. The market these days is shifting from fresh milk to dairy products like ghee, paneer, and yoghurt, which is driven by urbanisation and rising income. In this blog, you will learn about the dairy ecosystem, so keep reading.  

The Milk Production And Indian Dairy Ecosystem 

India has been the largest milk-producing and consuming country. The annual production was around 190 million tons annually. The prominent Indian dairy market includes Amul, Mother Dairy, Verka, and many more. India exports around 100,000 tonnes of dairy products and imports 50,000 tonnes. The trades are influenced by the tariffs. There has been a change in demand for cheese and other dairy products. The dairy industry also faces the challenges of milk tightening supply and rising procurement costs. The geopolitical tensions are also disrupting production patterns, especially in the northern milk-producing regions. The India-US trade agreement safeguards the interests of dairy farmers, providing significant protection of the dairy sector. It also aims to increase exports to expand the export market access in the United States. 

Dairy Trade Landscape: The global landscape for milk products like cheese, whey protein and maize is characterised by the demand for high protein and specialised dairy products. Cheese and whey protein are leading in the trade, and maize is dominant for animal feeding and an industrial crop. The demand for high-protein and health-focused foods is going to increase in the market.  The trade focus is shifting heavily towards cheese and whey protein ingredients as they are used in sports nutrition, medical foods, and specialised snacks. The global cheese markets are rising due to increased demand in Asia and emerging markets. While the demand for traditional dairy products is softening, protein and cheese are increasing. The sustainability and reduction of food waste are the by-products of the market to maximise value from whey. 

Conclusion 

In conclusion, the demand for dairy products is well supplied, and it keeps the prices stable in the competitive market by putting pressure on producers to invest in efficient, high-value processing technologies. For the best dairy processing machines, you can visit NK Dairy Equipment, which is a one-stop solution for the best technological dairy processing technologies, where our expert Mrs Himpreet Kaur provides the best experience.

FAQ’s 

  • Does the dairy equipment meet the safety standards?

Yes, the equipment is made with the best quality materials, as there should be no compromise with the food quality. 

  • Do you only provide equipment needed by the small-scale industries?

We provide the equipment to small dairy farming industries as well as to large-scale industries according to their needs and requirements.

title.rendered: How Can Khoya Be Prepared With Perfection At Home? Read To Find Out

content.rendered:
Khoya is one of the most demanded ingredients when it comes to deliciousness. No matter whether you use it in sweet dishes like gulab jamun, barfi, halwas or in spicy dishes like sahi paneer or koftas. It adds up a level of rich texture, excellent nutritious value and creamy flavour. For its preparation, most people use traditional methods which take high energy, excess manual work and large processing time. The traditional methods, even after excessive hard work, can give you inconsistent khoya. Sometimes, you have to compromise on the quality as well. This is a common problem in most households. In order to prepare khoya with extreme perfection and high quality, you need to work with a khoya-making machine. It also comes in a small size, which can fit easily in your home. If you are someone looking to learn about khoya-making machines, go through this blog thoroughly.

Understand the aspects of khoya making.

If you want to see the full potential of khoya and milk, you need to work with a machine. It will not only give you a high-quality khoya. But also increase quantity and take minimal to no manual work. There is a proper criterion for using the machine as well because even a small mistake can compromise the quality. Although once you understand the concept, it will be saved in your memory due to its super-simple and sequential functioning. Read the following crucial points to understand this properly. 

Machine preparation: Make sure you clean the machine before preparation. In order to maintain hygiene and smooth working. High-quality machines also come with self-cleaning functionality. This step is the most crucial factor to maintain taste and quality. It is advisable to use a good quality detergent for cleaning that is safe to maintain the quality. 

Processing: Pour the milk into the machine. Make sure you use full-fat milk. Simply turn on the machine to heat the milk. The machine stirrer will perfectly mix the milk. This is the most difficult task in the traditional method to perfectly mix the milk continuously until the milk reaches a certain stage. 

Perfection: Change the heat setting to medium. The machine will evaporate the water content within an hour. This is super simple to operate. Now, you have to wait until the milk becomes creamy like a dough. After this step, just cool it in the refrigerator inside an air-tight container. 

Note: You should be familiar with the quantity of milk you are using. This is important to understand how much hoya is obtained. For example, 250-500 grams can be obtained from 1 litre of milk. 

Conclusion 

The above-mentioned points clearly demonstrate the easy and effective procedure for the production of khoya using a good-quality machine. It is equally important to select a good-quality khoya machine for the smooth working of the entire process. The hygiene of the machine does not only come with cleaning but also selecting a machine made of food-grade stainless steel. There are multiple types of khoya machines available, depending on dairy or home use. If you are also looking for the same, you can also consider connecting with NK Dairy Equipment. Our expert, Himpreet Kaur, will ensure that the quality of the equipment is of high quality and fully professional.

Faqs

1. How often should I maintain the Khoya-making machine?

Well, the machine should be washed whenever you are using it. Professional maintenance is recommended to be done after 6 months to ensure the consistency of smooth working. 

2. How much time does it take to make khoya with a machine?

The process of making khoya with a machine is much faster than making it with traditional methods. The traditional methods can take more than two hours, but with khoya-making machines, it is only a matter of 15 to 30 minutes per batch.

After this step, just cool it in the refrigerator inside an air-tight container.  Note: You should be familiar with the quantity of milk you are using. This is important to understand how much hoya is obtained. For example, 250-500 grams can be obtained from 1 litre of milk.  Conclusion  The above-mentioned points clearly demonstrate the easy and effective procedure for the production of khoya using a good-quality machine. It is equally important to select a good-quality khoya machine for the smooth working of the entire process. The hygiene of the machine does not only come with cleaning but also selecting a machine made of food-grade stainless steel. There are multiple types of khoya machines available, depending on dairy or home use. If you are also looking for the same, you can also consider connecting with NK Dairy Equipment. Our expert, Himpreet Kaur, will ensure that the quality of the equipment is of high quality and fully professional. Faqs 1. How often should I maintain the Khoya-making machine? Well, the machine should be washed whenever you are using it. Professional maintenance is recommended to be done after 6 months to ensure the consistency of smooth working.  2. How much time does it take to make khoya with a machine? The process of making khoya with a machine is much faster than making it with traditional methods. The traditional methods can take more than two hours, but with khoya-making machines, it is only a matter of 15 to 30 minutes per batch.

title.rendered: Why Are the Solar-Powered Khoya Machines Beneficial For Production? Read Along To Know

content.rendered:
The solar-powered Khoya machines are what producers need when it comes to saving power, even under large-scale production. Unlike other machines that utilise wood, gas or electricity in Khoya production, this type of machine uses sunlight to generate power for production. Transitioning to this type of power supply has helped producers to significantly cut off power costs whilst also reducing labour compared to the traditional method of making khoya. It can be used for different capacities while minimally affecting the power supply. This blog elaborates on what the solar-powered khoya-making machine is all about, how it functions and its advantages. 

A solar-powered Khoya-making machine 

The solar-powered Khoya-making machine functions efficiently by utilising solar power and the inverter for heating, which is an effective method in saving power, especially for higher production. 

Solar-powered Khoya-making machine features 

Energy source: The Khoya machine has its power sourced from sunlight, where the solar panels draw light, and it is converted from DC to AC through the inverter. 

Components: The machines comprise components such as solar inverters, solar modules, the battery bank and the induction machine. 

Design: It is made from stainless steel for convenient hygiene purposes. 

How does the solar-powered Khoya-making machine work? 

The solar-powered Khoya-making machine requires a number of components to  function effectively, and these are 

Solar panels: The solar panels draw sunlight that is converted. 

Solar inverter: This is used to convert the sunlight into power used for heating the machine. 

Power distribution system: After the power is converted, it is distributed to the induction unit.

Optional battery storage: The battery is important when using it at night or for any other backup operations. 

Advantages 

Lower energy costs: The solar-powered Khoya machine utilises sunlight power that requires little to no electricity, hence cutting down on electricity expenses, especially for large-scale production. 

Enhanced efficiency: The modern solar-powered Khoya machines automate stirring and heating processes, which reduces production time and also saves power. 

Improved product quality: The improved heating system improves the quality of the products by avoiding the burning of the milk and ensuring even heating. 

Reliable in different areas: The solar-powered Khoya can be used in rural areas because it does not rely on electricity. 

Versatile and scalable: The solar-powered Khoya making machine is available for different capacities, and it is used for different products, making it easy for producers to scale up if needed. 

Conclusion 

The solar-powered Khoya-making machine is there to reduce expenses for large production. Producers can reduce expenses and, at the same time, increase production if needed, whilst still saving power. Not only does it save power, but it is free from boiler operation, which is therefore significantly cost-effective and has lower risks. NK Dairy Equipment is your trusted supplier for the solar-powered Khoya-making machines. Mrs. Himpreet Kaur and the team of experts help clients get high-quality dairy equipment with the right capacity. Our products are guaranteed, and we ensure that clients are helped in selecting the right machines for their production. 

FAQ’s 

  1. Is the solar-powered Khoya machine labour-saving?

Yes, the solar-powered khoya machine saves labour. It has installed automatic systems, such as automatic heating systems and automatic stirring systems, which eliminate the need for labour to consistently stir the milk, and even the production of heat eliminates the need for consistent monitoring for heat supply for high-quality production.

  1. What capacities are available for the solar-powered khoya machine?

Small-scale productions can range from 50 to 100 litres, medium-scale productions can handle from 100 to 300 litres, and larger scales are for bigger production exceeding 500 litres.

title.rendered: Why New FSSAI Labelling Rules Matter For Dairy Plant Owners?

content.rendered:
While looking for the best dairy processing equipment for your dairy plants, the inclusion of modern features is not the only factor that should be considered. In fact, it is necessary to look for new FSSAI labelling rules as well that are significantly aligned with consumer safety and health standards. Such labelling rules include the nutritional value, along with specific milk product logos that can efficiently help in fulfilling the consumers’ needs and nutritional gaps. While selecting the dairy processing machines, such as the khoya machine, milk making machine, along with packaging the milk products, dairy plant owners should prioritise the inclusion of new FSSAI labelling rules with the aim of preventing consumers from contaminated milk products. This holds significant importance for the dairy plant owners so that they will get to know about the specific dairy machine for processing different dairy products without compromising their quality and safety. If you are willing to know more regarding the importance of new labelling rules for dairy plant owners, then make sure to go through this blog in an essential manner. 

Importance of new FSSAI labelling rules for dairy plant owners. 

New FSSAI labelling rules hold utmost importance for dairy plant owners in terms of providing a guarantee regarding the nutritional benefits of the processed dairy products in an essential manner. Dairy plant owners play an integral role in producing milk products and packaging them with precision and accuracy to meet the consumer’s health standards. Therefore, it is necessary for them to align with the new FSSAI labelling rules. However, to know more regarding its importance for fairy plant owners, go through the points mentioned below depicting the same. 

Provide accurate guidance to consumers: One of the major importance of following the new FSSAI labelling rules for the dairy plant owners includes the mentioning of specific nutritional information, which significantly depicts different nutrients added to the specific milk products. This can efficiently help in guiding the consumers regarding the nutritional value of the particular milk product, thus providing them with accurate information regarding the inclusion of different nutrients in the milk products, and preventing them from being misled. 

Prevent strict penalties: Another major importance of following the new labelling rules for the dairy plant owners is the prevention of strict and legal penalties. If a dairy plant owner does not follow the new labelling rules, then they can be charged with heavy penalties, thus destroying their reputational image as well. However, following all the necessary labelling rules can prevent them from such circumstances in an essential manner. 

Fosters customers’ trust: Winning customers’ trust is one of the strongest pillars for dairy plant owners in terms of making them run in this competitive world. Therefore, if the dairy plant owners ensure to follow all the new FSSAI labelling rules, including the mentioning of nutritional value, use of specific ingredients and different product identification, then customers would foster their credibility in the specific dairy plant. This can even make them feel like their health concern is prioritised by the inclusion of all the labelling rules in the packaging. 

Conclusion 

New FSSAI labelling rules hold major importance in terms of educating consumers regarding the nutritional value of the specific milk product, along with providing them with a brief product description. This built their trust in the specific dairy plant industry in an essential manner. The above-mentioned points vividly demonstrate the importance of following the new labelling rules for the dairy plant owners in terms of preventing them from legal penalties, along with providing safe and tested milk products to the consumers. Along with this, make sure to engage with the best dairy processing manufacturer that understands the necessity of all new labelling rules. If you are looking for one in India, then you can engage with NK Dairy Equipment, following all the safety standards for the prevention of contamination in dairy products. Our experts, Mrs Himpreet Kaur, ensure to guide you regarding the necessary food safety standards for providing high-quality dairy processing machines to the dairy plant owners. 

FAQs

  1. What is the meaning of QR code in the packaging of milk products?

The inclusion of QR codes in all milk product packaging includes the information of all the labelling requirements in different languages so that consumers can better understand. 

  1. What if the dairy plant owner does not follow the new labelling rules?

If the dairy plant owner does not follow the new labelling rules, then it can force them to pay heavy fines, along with the suspension of the FSSAI license in an essential manner.

title.rendered: What Are The Best Features To Look For In A Stainless Steel Khoya-Making Machine?

content.rendered:
Are you confused about making the right choice of a khoya-making machine for your small or large-scale industry? If so, don’t worry, choosing the perfect khoya-making machine can be made through checking and verifying some key points and functionality. The most important thing is to choose stainless steel for the khoya-making machine, which should feature high-grade stainless steel. It is important to choose high-grade steel for hygiene and safety. The latest khoya machines are equipped with an automated stirrer to prevent burning and a tilting mechanism for easy cleaning and product removal. The advanced models also provide precise electric heating control, efficient insulation to reduce the fuel costs and high-capacity production, ensuring consistent texture and saving of labour wages. Let’s discuss in detail what the best features you should look for while purchasing a steel khoya-making machine in this blog.

Below are the key features to prioritise

  • Material quality and durability

Ensure that the food contact surfaces are high-grade stainless steel SS 304 grade to comply with food safety standards. It helps to prevent corrosion and maintain hygiene while processing, and also ensures easy cleaning. A thicker stainless steel sheet allows for better heat retention and longevity, and is most appropriate for the commercial application.

  • Automation and efficiency

The automatic stirring mechanism is essential to prevent the khoya from burning at the bottom. It eliminates the labour stress of stirring and does the work more efficiently in less time. It ensures the uniform thickness of the Khoya without constant manual labour. You should look for a machine that allows you to control the speed of the stirrer to adapt to different stages of milk reduction. 

  • Operating features

A tilting mechanism is essential for the safe discharging of the khoya after the processing from the container, which reduces the manual labour and spills. You should choose between the efficient gas burners, diesel or electric heating. The high-performance models often have uniform heat distribution, which promotes better processing. Go for good insulation machines as they reduce the heat loss, saving up to thirty to forty per cent on fuel costs. 

  • Control and safety

You should choose the machine with digital temperature control, as it provides you with the freedom to adjust the temperature according to the phase of the processing. It offers accurate temperature control, minimising the risks of both overheating or underheating, which ensures a high-quality product in every batch without compromising the taste. It should also have systematic cooking cycles, which can contribute to the consistent quality of the Khoya and free up staff to handle other tasks effectively. 

Conclusion

The best stainless steel khoya machine is the one constructed from high-grade steel, which is necessary for long-lasting, hygienic and efficient performance. It should also include a robust heating system, an automated stirring mechanism and a tilting design for easy discharge. Investing in a machine with accurate temperature controls ensures consistent quality in each and every batch. Along with this, it reduces the energy consumption, making it ideal for high-volume dairy industries and sweet shops. By purchasing the Khoya making machine from NK Dairy Equipment, you ensure the best quality and most efficient khoya production in every batch, which will be directly proven by our customer reviews on our products. Our expert Ms. Himpreet Kaur, keeps us motivated to provide the best quality equipment to our customers to build a friendly relationship between the businesses. 

FAQs

1. How does a khoya machine prevent the milk from burning?

The machine is equipped with a continuously operating automated stirrer that ensures the milk does not get stuck to the bottom and burn.

2. Can I use the same machine for making different types of Khoya?

Yes, by controlling the temperature and stirring time, you can make different textures of khoya in the same machine. The various consistencies of the soya could be smooth, grainy or a form that can be made with this machine.

3. What is the estimated production capacity of these machines?

The production capacity can vary according to the size and needs of the industry. Its capacity can range from processing twenty litres per hour to a hundred litres per hour, depending on the size of the industry.

title.rendered: Worried About The LPG Shortage In Dairy Industries? Here Are Some Best Alternatives To Keep Operations Running

content.rendered:
Have you ever wondered about the impact of the current LPG shortage on dairy industries? If not, then it is necessary to vividly understand the harmful impact of LPG shortage in terms of creating a disruption in the process of heating the milk. Along with this, the sterilisation of dairy equipment is also impacted, thus creating an obstacle in the smooth operations of dairy industries. This is where dairy industries need to adapt to other alternatives that can play a major role in resuming the smooth operation of the processing of dairy products. Make sure to use PNG, Induction-based heating and biogas that can significantly help in heating the milk with the aim of removing all the bacteria, along with sterilising the equipment in an effective manner. Also, it is crystal clear that there’s a high need for dairy industries to diversify their energy sources with the purpose of keeping their operations running smoothly. To know more regarding the alternatives for LPG in dairy industries, make sure to go through this blog in an essential manner. 

Four best alternatives for LPG in dairy industries. 

Due to the LPG shortage in India, most of the dairy industries have witnessed a sudden downfall in their operations, thus making it difficult for them to make a high profit. This is where the best alternatives to LPG can play an integral role in terms of keeping the operations running. Considering this, go through the points mentioned below, highlighting the same in a thorough manner. 

Electric and Induction-based heating: One of the best alternatives to LPG includes the electric and induction-based heating, which is significantly used for heating milk in a precise manner in the dairy industries. The major benefit of opting for induction-based heating is that it is cheaper than LPG, which is costly nowadays in India. You can use electric/ induction-based heating for pasteurisation and other heating requirements in the process of producing dairy products. Small or medium-sized dairy industries can opt for this alternative with the purpose of keeping their operations running. 

Biogas from cow dung: Another best alternative to LPG in dairy industries include the use of converted cow dung and other waste in dairy farms into energy. This converted energy can be significantly used in fulfilling the essential heating requirements of dairy industries with the aim of producing dairy products in a precise manner. This can be the best choice for dairy farms with more than a hundred cattle, thus providing a cheap and cost-effective solution. 

Solar thermal energy: Dairy industries can even opt for production of what through the smarter technologies involved in solar thermal energy. This can significantly help with the process of milk pasteurisation in the dairy industry, with the purpose of producing different dairy products. Along with this, sterilising the dairy equipment can be done in an efficient manner in order to resume operations in the dairy industry. For the dairy industry’s location in the solar regions, this alternative would be the top choice. 

Use of firewood: Many dairy industries in India have been currently using firewood furnaces to fulfil the heating requirements for the short term. Firewood can be the best alternative to LPG in terms of completing the essential heating step required for the production of dairy products. Rural dairy industries have been actively using firewood for pasteurisation and heating the milk in an essential manner. 

Conclusion

LPG shortages have created a great buzz in the dairy industry in terms of disrupting the operation of heating the milk in a precise manner. Therefore, choosing the best and affordable alternatives to LPG storage, such as biogas, solar thermal energy and induction-based boilers, can play an integral role in the precise production of dairy products. The above-mentioned points vividly demonstrate the best alternatives that dairy industries can opt for. Also, make sure to engage with the best dairy equipment supplier in order to adapt the equipment to different energy sources for the production of dairy products. If you have been looking for the best dairy equipment supplier, make sure to engage with NK Dairy Equipment in India. Our expert, Mrs Himpreet Kaur, makes sure to provide you with high-quality dairy equipment that can align with different energy sources. 

FAQs

  1. Should I check my farm’s load capacity before opting for induction-based heating?

Yes, it is important to thoroughly check the load capacity of your dairy industry before opting for induction-based heating with the purpose of getting the precise results. 

  1. How can dairy equipment suppliers help with different alternatives to LPG in the dairy industry?

Dairy equipment suppliers can significantly help in adding the new technology and features to your current dairy equipment, with the aim of using other alternatives precisely.

title.rendered: How Do Modern Cream Separators Boost The Profitability For Ghee Producers?

content.rendered:
Modern cream separators rapidly boost profitability for ghee producers by maximising the fat content in the cream, which is achieved through the centrifugal force generated by the machine. It captures up to ninety-eight per cent of the fat compared to the traditional methods, which directly increases the yield of high-value cream from the same volume of milk. These machines are easily available from any popular dairy equipment manufacturer in India. This machine allows for precise standardisation of cream richness, which should contain forty to sixty per cent fat, resulting in consistent and premium quality ghee in every batch. This improvement in the quality and better production can increase the overall revenue of the small as well as large-scale industries. Moreover, efficient separation reduces fat loss and accelerates a faster rate of interest. Let’s discuss in detail the major ways modern cream separators can boost profitability for ghee producers in this blog.

Key profitability drivers

  • Maximised recovery and fat recovery: The modern separation processes the cream at high centrifugal force with continuous rotation of six thousand to ten thousand RPM. This process reduces the fat losses up to 0.05 per cent in skim milk. This process results in more cream and, therefore, more production of ghee from the same quantity of milk as compared to the traditional methods.
  • Reduced operational costs: The automated system minimises the need for manual labour which automatically reduces the operational costs. The efficient and consistent separation of the cream can reduce the energy consumption for heating by fifteen to thirty per cent.
  • Consistent quality and higher value: The milk contains the standard fat content of forty to sixty per cent cream when processed with modern cream separators. This ensures consistent quality and quantity of ghee produced from every batch. This indirectly increases customer loyalty and allows for premium pricing.
  • Faster processing: Modern separators can handle hundreds and thousands of litres per hour. It increases the processing speed, which saves time and reduces the need for labour. This fast processing yields drastically higher production compared to the traditional methods.
  • Valuable byproducts: This single process is used for producing ghee, and it is also suitable for producing high-quality skimmed milk that can be sold or used for other products. This approach ensures that each component of the milk is properly used which reduces the wastage and maximises the output.

Conclusion

Modern cream separators have become extremely necessary for the profitability of commercial ghee production. This process maximises fat recovery, reduces labour-intensive tasks and ensures consistent product quality throughout the automation. They allow the producers to increase the amount of material passing through the machine, which successfully achieves thirty per cent more output from the same quantity of raw milk. In spite of the fact that this set-up requires a high initial investment, installing cream separators can increase the efficiency of the cream production by maintaining ninety-six to ninety-eight per cent fat in cream, which ensures a rapid return on investment. By purchasing a cream separator from NK dairy equipment, we guarantee to provide quick installations of the machinery, either for small-scale or large-scale industry, under the guidance of our expert Mrs. Himpreet Kaur. This equipment will act as an important part of the profitability and your scalable dairy business. 

FAQs

  1. What is the ideal cream fat percentage for ghee production?

It is generally recommended to set the cream separator to a fat content between forty per cent and sixty per cent for the best results. 

  1. Can a cream separator work on homogenised milk?

No, the homogenisation process reduces the fat globules to a size that makes it impossible for a standard cream separator to separate the cream from the skimmed milk. 

  1. How often should I clean the cream separator?

The machinery should be disassembled and cleaned properly after use to prevent bacterial contamination and to maintain hygiene by complying with the safety standards for food. Always use food equipment-specific detergent to clean the equipment.

title.rendered: Why Dairy Equipment Suppliers Hold Major Importance For Dairy Farms?

content.rendered:
Back in the time when there was less innovation in the dairy industry, the dairy farmers underwent various struggles in terms of milk extraction. Due to manual labour in dairy farms, the risk of contamination was high, along with this, the productivity was significantly impacted. However, in today’s times, the dairy industry has developed rapidly, which has become a boon to many dairy farms. The inclusion of different dairy equipment has eased the lives of many farmers by reducing the burden of performing manual duties while extracting milk. With the help of milking machines, the farmers can easily extract the milk without the need to rely on the shoulders of labourers. Furthermore, dairy farms can get exceptional and effective benefits by engaging with the reputed dairy equipment suppliers to ease their regular struggle. To know more regarding the importance of dairy equipment suppliers for dairy farms, go through this blog in an essential manner. 

Importance of a dairy equipment supplier for dairy farms.

Due to an ease in milk extraction and increased productivity, dairy farms can effortlessly rely on dairy equipment suppliers. Considering this, go through the points mentioned below, highlighting the importance of dairy equipment suppliers for dairy farms, so keep reading. 

Preserves milk quality: Dairy equipment suppliers excel in providing farmers with chillers that can significantly help them in storing the milk efficiently. By engaging with the best dairy equipment suppliers, the farmers can ensure that their milk will remain preserved in a hygienic environment without coming into contact with any type of bacteria. Chillers are made up of stainless steel that can efficiently help in protecting the milk quality, thus promoting safety and hygiene. 

Provides high revenue: Manual labour can negatively impact milk production, which can even result in lower revenues. However, by engaging with dairy equipment suppliers, the farmers can get access to different dairy processing machines that are highly durable. Due to such machines, the farmers can manage their milk production, which can result in significantly higher revenue. By producing a large quantity of milk without impacting its quality, dairy farms can get maximum profit through the markets. 

Smooth operational flow: Dairy equipment suppliers provide high-quality dairy machines that excel in preparing different dairy products such as ghee and khoya. Due to their durable and high-quality features, the chances of machines getting damaged are reduced, thus saving the costly and frequent repairs. Along with this, with the comprehensive utilisation of dairy processing equipment, the wastage of resources is reduced, thus providing a smooth operational flow. The farmers can continue to process dairy products without any disruption or trouble. 

Enhanced productivity with low labour cost: The inclusion of dairy equipment in the dairy farms can significantly boost their milk productivity by eliminating all the hassles that the farmers were facing while extracting milk with the help of labourers. Along with this, the need for labourers gets reduced as almost all the tasks are efficiently performed by different dairy processing machines. Therefore, the farmers can witness a hike in their productivity by relying on several dairy equipment items. 

Conclusion

Dairy processing machines provided by dairy equipment suppliers can play a crucial role in enhancing the productivity of specific dairy farms, along with improving milk quality. The above-mentioned points vividly demonstrate the importance of engaging with the best dairy equipment suppliers for your dairy farms in terms of increasing your output in an effective manner. If you are looking for the best dairy equipment supplier in India, then make sure to engage with NK Dairy Equipments. Our expert, Mrs. Himpreet Kaur, ensures that you hand-pick the right type of dairy processing machines that truly align with your specific needs in order to manage your productivity.

Dairy equipment suppliers provide different milking equipment such as milking machines, vacuum pumps and pulsators with high precision.

title.rendered: Why Are Milking Equipment Essential for the Dairy Industry?

content.rendered:
In large-scale dairy industries, there is a bulk of milk to be processed to make various dairy products. The processing of the huge quantity of milk is not possible manually. It required specialised equipment to process the milk for dairy product production such as khoya and ghee. The first step for making these products is extracting the milk from the cow, which is extracted in large quantities. For this extraction of milk, dairy industries use milking equipment. This equipment is essential to the modern dairy industry for significantly increasing efficiency, ensuring high hygienic standards and maximising milk yield. By automating the process of milk extraction, these machines save time and reduce labour dependency. This provides consistent extraction of milk while maintaining the herd’s health. It also reduces the risk of contamination of milk compared to other procedures. Now, let’s discuss in detail the reasons behind the importance of milking equipment in dairy industries in this blog. 

Below are the five key reasons for its essentiality:

  • Efficiency and speed

There is no doubt in believing that when manual work is replaced by machine-automated work, it is definitely more efficient and faster. When milking machines are used in place of manual labour, they provide an adequate amount of milk extraction in the most optimal time. The machines allow milking multiple animals at once, which increases the speed of the extraction and promotes faster milk production. 

  • Hygiene and quality

In an automated system, as every work is done by machine hands, there is no risk of contamination from human hands. Machines for food industries are made with a self-cleaning feature to maintain the best quality food in every batch, which was not possible when done manually. This leads to a better milk quality and reduced spoilage due to any contamination and indirectly provides improved food safety.

  • Increased productivity

Automated machines provide consistent milk extraction, which increases the production of milk in less time. Consistent and complete milking ensures the maximum milk removal. It stimulates more consistent milk yields per cow and allows for data collection. Increased productivity results in more profit and less downtime. 

  • Animal welfare

The milking machine is designed in a way that mimics the natural feeling of the manual extraction of milk. It mimics the natural sucking action of the calves. It does so by using vacuum pressure and rhythmic massage to prevent heat damage and reduce animal stress. It is specially designed with flexible liners to prevent injury to the calf.  

  • Reduced labour costs

When most of the work is done by the machines in the dairy industry, the need for labour automatically gets reduced. If the labourers are fewer, then there are fewer expenses spent to pay the labourers, as investment is made in the machines. This helps to reduce labour costs as there is less labour left that has to be paid. It makes operations more economically maintained and enables high-volume production. 

Conclusion

Milking equipment is essential to the modern dairy industry. This is because it automates the manual process of milk extraction, making it highly efficient for high-volume production of milk. These modern technologies are important for ensuring high-quality, hygienic milk while prioritising the herd’s health through stress-free extraction. This reduces the dependence on manual labour by improving milking speed. The machinery boosts productivity and enhances profitability, along with providing better data-driven herd management. As the industry moves towards larger herd sizes and stricter safety standards, it is becoming essential to invest in milking equipment. Nk Dairy Equipments provides you with the best quality milking, cooling and storage equipment, which is necessary to maintain a competitive edge and maintain operation. Our products follow all the food safety standards as ensured by our expert, Mrs. Himpreet Kaur. 

FAQs

  1. Are milking machines safe for milk extraction from cows?

Yes, milking machines are extremely safe and comfortable for cows when they are properly maintained and used. Along with this, they can even reduce the risk of injuries as compared to manual labour.

  1. How often should milking machine liners be replaced?

It is generally suggested to replace the liner after every fifteen hundred to twenty-five hundred milkings, or roughly after every six months, to ensure effective sanitary operation.

title.rendered: Do You Know About Industrial Dairy Equipment? Let’s Get Familiar With Its Concept And Features!

content.rendered:
Industrial dairy equipment incorporates specialised machinery made of stainless steel, which is designed for the hygienic collection, processing, and packaging of dairy products on a large scale. The main equipment includes milk reception units, homogenisers for the improvement of texture, pasteurisers for the removal of bacteria, storage tanks, packaging machines and separation units as well. This ensures the quality of the product as well as its safety from farm to your table. These systems allow for quicker processing, reduction in labour dependence, as well as consistent production of a number of dairy items such as butter, cheese and yoghurt. 

Core components of industrial dairy processing equipment 

  • Milk collection as well as reception: This includes milk pumps, weight bowls, tankers, as well as receiving tanks that are able to handle raw milk just upon arrival at the processing plant.
  • Separators as well as Clarifiers: Separators and clarifiers refer to the centrifugal machines that are known to remove impurities, thereby separating milk into cream, skimmed milk or even standardised fat content.
  • Pasteurisers: These are the equipment which are responsible for heating the milk to specific temperatures in order to eliminate the pathogens which are present in it, thereby increasing its shelf life. This is very crucial for safety purposes.
  • Homogenisers: The high-pressure machines that generally break down the globules of fat, thereby creating a consistent texture, are referred to as homogenisers. Moreover, they also prevent cream separation.
  • Storage tanks and Cooling tanks: They are the insulated tanks, which are made of stainless steel, like bulk milk coolers, that usually maintain low temperatures for the purpose of preserving the quality of milk.
  • Processing Units: The processing units are the specialised tanks which are used in the making of yoghurt, cheese or even curd, which usually feature control of temperature for the process of fermentation.
  • Packaging Machinery: The machinery is an automated machine which is used for drilling cartons, pouches, and bottles.
  • Cleaning in Place systems – CIP: CIP refers to the automated equipment for sanitisation purposes that is used to circulate the cleaning agents through pipes and tanks in order to maintain hygiene without disassembly.

Vital Features 

  • Material: The material of the industrial dairy equipment is almost exclusively food-grade stainless steel in order to prevent corrosion, thereby ensuring sanitation.
  • Automation: In order to control temperature, flow and pressure, modern and high-speed lines usually make use of integrated and automated systems.
  • Maintenance: This includes strict cleaning protocols, which are necessary in order to prevent contamination. Maintenance often uses specialised valves as well as piping for more efficient cleaning.

Conclusion  

The industrial dairy equipment ensures uniform processing, such as precise control of temperature during the process of pasteurisation or homogenisation, which results in consistent quality, texture and taste of the product. Many types of modern systems are allowing dairies to scale up their production in order to meet the growing market demand without compromising the quality of equipment. You can buy the best and high-quality products from NK Dairy Equipment. Himpreet Kaur ensures you receive the great quality of the products at affordable prices. 

FAQs 

Q1: In dairy, what is a CIP system? 

A1: CIP – Cleaning-in-Place refers to a method which is used in cleaning the interior surfaces of vessels, pipes, and process equipment without dismantling them by making use of heat, water and a few chemicals. 

Q2: Which piece of equipment is considered to be the most important in a dairy farm? 

A2: The milking machine is that piece of equipment which is considered the most critical and important because it is used more hours every year as compared to any other kind of equipment in order to efficiently and safely harvest milk.

title.rendered: How does Automated Dairy Processing Technology Provide Significant Benefits to the Manufacturers? Read to Find Out

content.rendered:
In order to produce the dairy products, most of the manufacturers have opted for automated dairy processing technology to get the profitable benefits. The automation in dairy processing products has effectively overcome the traditional methods of preparing dairy products. Such automation has become a boon to the manufacturers in terms of easing their complex tasks of preparing dairy products and reducing human error. Due to the automated dairy processing technology, manufacturers can witness the amazing benefits, which include increased operational efficiency and profitable results. Therefore, choosing automation in dairy processing products can be a wise decision for the manufacturers, hence removing the complexities involved in the preparation of dairy products. In order to get familiar with the benefits of automation in dairy processing products, read this blog in an attentive manner. 

Benefits of automation in dairy processing products for manufacturers. 

Manufacturers can get amazing benefits from the automation in dairy processing products by providing them with higher output and effectively increasing their productivity. To know more regarding this, go through the points mentioned below in a thorough manner. 

Exceptional product quality: The automation in dairy processing products allows the preparation of dairy products in an enhanced and exceptional manner. The dairy products produced by the dairy processing machines are of enhanced quality, proper texture and flavourful taste. Automation includes the accurate precision parameters, such as temperature and pH level, in order to prepare an enhanced quality product. This can significantly reduce resource wastage, hence beneficial for the manufacturers. 

Inclusion of food safety standards: Due to the automation in dairy processing products, the need for human intervention is effectively reduced, which can prevent the risk of contamination. Due to the production of such hygienic dairy products, customers can build their trust with the specific manufacturers, therefore, providing benefits to them. Along with this, automated dairy processing technology involves proper sanitation, which ensures that the dairy products are produced in a safe and hygienic place, hence providing benefits to the manufacturers in terms of gaining customers’ reliability. 

Effectively protect the workers: Automation decreases the need to perform repetitive or hazardous tasks, which can prevent the workers from injuries. As a result of this, the workers remain protected throughout the process of producing dairy products. This can even allow the workers to focus on high-value tasks that can extract the maximum profit for the manufacturers. 

Accelerate productivity: Due to automation, the dependency on manual labour has been reduced, along with their wages. Along with this, the unnecessary wastage of resources is also reduced as automation allows the usage of resources without any wastage. Consequently, this can reduce the operational costs, hence increasing productivity. 

Conclusion

Automation can improve the process of producing dairy products by effectively eliminating the need for human error and automating repetitive tasks. Automated dairy processing technology includes the enhanced quality of dairy products with the perfect texture, consistency and taste. Along with this, meeting the food safety standards due to the automation in dairy processing products can significantly boost the reputation of the specific manufacturer. The above-mentioned points clearly demonstrate the benefits of automation in dairy processing products for the manufacturers, hence increasing their operational efficiency. Considering this, NK Dairy Equipments offers automated dairy equipment that can effectively help in easing the complex tasks of producing dairy products. The entire process of the production of dairy products is apparently monitored by Mrs. Himpreet Kaur, hence providing the optimum results at the end.

FAQs

  1. Does automation help with the shortage of labour in dairy processing products?

Yes, automation can significantly help in allowing manufacturers to continue production even with a few workers, as it helps in reducing repetitive and hazardous tasks. 

     2.  Does automation provide energy consumption solutions?

Yes, automation helps in reducing power consumption without impacting the product quality. Due to the automated systems, you can witness the preparation of dairy products without the utilization of much energy.

title.rendered: Are You Familiar With The Concept Of Rethinking Water? Let’s Get Familiar With It!

content.rendered:
The concept of rethinking water refers to an innovative water management approach. Sustainable food security needs a fundamental shift in how we manage precious water by recognising that it is getting scarce and moreover, that it is a crucial resource which is essential for food processing, agriculture as well as human survival, specifically with demand planned to surpass supply, thereby entailing improvements such as efficient waste water systems and furthermore, reusing that water to reduce strain on freshwater. It projects that even though water seems abundant, it is a serious bottleneck for feeding this much growing population, thereby affecting everything, from growing several crops to packaging our food. 

For processing food efficiently concerning water, it is important for the industries to emphasise not just water reduction but also recycling and reusing this water throughout the complete cycle of production. This incorporates adoption of new technologies and approaches, changing functional behaviours and enhancing processes – everything to minimise the overall water footprint of the production of food. 

Why rethinking water is crucial 

  • Scarcity of water: The demand for freshwater is expected to exceed the supply of water globally by 40% by the year 2030, thereby creating massive pressure on food and beverage industries.
  • Steps on the Value Chain: Water is essential as it is integral to agriculture, for the growth of food and also for processing food, such as for the process of cooling, cleaning and packaging, as well, thereby making its responsible management crucial.
  • Functional Efficiency: People are finding major savings by executing smarter water use.
  • Adapting to climate change: As the climate is changing year by year, future climate scenarios suggest increased amounts of water deficits, thereby making the reuse of water and integrated management crucial for food supplies in future.

The process of rethinking water 

  • Innovation: It involves implementing modern advanced technologies, such as efficient treatment for wastewater, and reusing the systems in the production of food.
  • Water Stewardship: It incorporates shifting beyond functions for the purpose of collaborating on the responsible use of water across entire regions globally.
  • Integrated management: This encompasses the designing of system-wide water portfolios that are in combination with reservoir management, allocation, and also reuse in order to meet the diverse needs.
  • Shifting Mindsets: It is crucial to recognise the true value of water, which is the same as the diamond water paradox, for ascertaining sustainable availability for ecosystems, communities and business.

Core strategies for efficient water use in the processing of food 

  • Water audits:
  • Treatment of wastewater
  • Recycling water and reusing
  • Automation and precise control
  • Modern design of equipment
  • Waterless alternatives
  • Optimised production scheduling
  • Management of water quality

Conclusion 

With the help of the implementation of the above-mentioned strategies, it is possible for the food industry to significantly reduce the functional costs, thereby lessening the strain on local as well as global water supplies and enhancing the overall sustainability of the environment. NK Dairy Equipments provides you with all the tools and machinery for your needs. Mrs Himpreet Kaur works towards supplying the best quality equipment for dairy needs. You can buy water RO plants and other dairy processing-related plants. We are trusted by more than 10,000 clients. And you can be one of them. Visit us today and buy for your specific needs. 

FAQs 

Q1: How can we manage water scarcity? 

A:   You can manage water scarcity by implementing water-efficient technologies such as smart metering and recycling water in processes, thereby optimising workflows and enhancing irrigation on farms. 

Q2: What are the biggest challenges in rethinking water? 

A:    The biggest challenges are scarcity, ageing infrastructure, pollution – metals, pesticides and pathogens, energy costs and climate change impacts.

title.rendered: What Are The Advantages Of The Quadro Ytron ZC for Non-Dairy Milk? Read Along To Know

content.rendered:
The Quadro Ytron ZC for non-dairy milk is a game changer for manufacturers because it offers superior powder dispersion, single pass efficiency, time and cost savings and flexibility, which helps to meet the demands of the non-dairy milk consumers. The ZC is an invention that is designed to process different ingredients for milk production. The engineering of this machine can process to disperse the most difficult to wet powder and the thickening agents into a smooth, liquid stream in a single streamline pass. This invention has elevated non-dairy milk production. This blog eludes the advantages of the Quadro Ytron ZC for non-dairy milk.

Reasons for choosing the Quadro Ytron ZC For Non-Dairy Milk

Superior powder dispersion 

The dispenser of the Quadro Ytron ZC is uniquely designed to handle the thickening agents and powders to ensure that it is completely free of lumps, creating a harmonious mixture. Hence, the powder is smooth with excellent filtration. 

Single pass efficiency

The machine is efficient in single-pass powder dispersion, which is a process where complex powders are mixed into the liquid stream in a single continuous operation that in turn produces a homogeneous mixture. This process eliminates the need for tank additions, which results in making the process lengthy and slow. 

Eliminates air entrainment 

Through different methods, the Quadro Ytron ZC is able to prevent aeration. Firstly, the machine has a near-perfect vacuum, which has a high-speed rotor that creates a significant vacuum in the reactor head.  This sucks the powder directly into the liquid stream. The ZC processes the mixture inside the piping, which keeps the entire process enclosed. 

Significant time and cost savings 

Unlike the traditional system, the Quadro Ytron ZC has proven to be time efficient and cost-effective. It reduces the overall batch times by a high percentage. For example, complex processes that are supposed to take ten hours are reduced to less than an hour. This allows faster and more effective production. The single pass efficiency, the minimized deaeration, and all processes that effectively reduce production time also reduce costs. 

Enhanced product quality

The machine produces premium quality products. It ensures a lump-free product by eliminating the clumps of powder, as it intensely shears the powder before hydrating it. The machine ensures optimal viscosity, and the single-pass process achieves high viscosity through stretching, which in turn ensures a consistent viscosity throughout each batch. 

Sanitary design

The machine meets the sanitary standards. It is easy to clean, the surfaces and joints are reachable and easy to clean. The machine is made of stainless steel, which is non-reactive and durable. It is suitable for Clean In Place (CIP) and Sterilisation-In-Place. This allows the machine to be cleaned thoroughly without being disassembled. 

Flexibility and scalability 

It has interchangeable rotors with different slot widths. This allows the manufacturers to tailor the shear intensity to specific ingredients. The machine allows multiple delivery options, which include manual hoppers, ergonomic dump tables, suction lances and large-scale bulk bags. Lastly, a single portable ZC unit can serve multiple tanks, which makes it flexible. 

Conclusion 

The Quadro Ytron ZC for non-dairy milk elevates the overall quality of production. Investing in it would be a doorway to premium production and flexibility for the manufacturers. If you are planning on investing in milk processing plants NK Dairy Equipment led by Himpreet Kaur will get the job done excellently. Our team of experts ensures that they provide and install dairy equipment that is tailored to the customer’s requirements. Be assured that the machines are of high quality, the customer service is smooth, and the company ensures that it understands the needs of customers in order to deliver excellent services. 

FAQ’s 

  1. Does the Quadro Ytron ZC for non-dairy milk handle high solid concentrations?

The Quadro Ytron ZC for non-dairy milk is designed to handle high concentrations, and it achieves a solid dispersion. It also has high control accuracy with achievable inline concentrations. 

  1. Is it okay for shear-sensitive in a Quadro Ytron ZC for non-dairy milk?

The Quadro Ytron ZC for non-dairy milk is designed to be process sensitive. Its unique processing protects shear-sensitive ingredients through the single-pass process.

title.rendered: Is Precision Cooling Crucial For Meeting Global Milk Quality Standards? Know About It Now

content.rendered:
Precision milk cooling is an essential step for maintaining milk quality after extraction. It is essential because freshly extracted milk is extremely sensitive to bacterial growth if kept at 37 degrees Celsius or room temperature. Precision cooling helps to prevent that bacterial growth. If the milk is kept unchecked at room temperature, it will start producing bacteria rapidly. This is done by using a Bulk Milk Cooling Plant that can cool down a huge amount of milk at once and is used in most of the dairy farms. Along with these procedures, the cows’ health also plays a vital role in maintaining the milk quality. This process is done to meet the milk quality standard that is determined by whole milk composition (fats, protein, solids) and metrics like SPC (Standard plate count) and SCC (Somatic cell count). Let’s discuss it through this blog.

THE SCIENCE BEHIND THE PRECISION COOLING

A cow produces milk at 38 degrees Celsius, which is most suitable for bacterial breeding. Instead of Traditional cooling methods that take so long to reach the desired cooling temperature, Precision cooling methods must be used, as they instantly cool the milk to four degrees Celsius, preventing bacterial growth that might occur abruptly in traditional methods.

PRESERVES THE NUTRITIONAL VALUE

According to the FSSAI milk standard, full cream milk must contain more than six percent fat and more than 9 percent SNF(Solid-Not-Fat), and this percentage differs for other types of milk. Not meeting these nutritional value standards proves poor milk quality. For maintaining this nutritional value, precision cooling brings stability to it. Even minor fluctuations in temperature can cause the breakdown of essential nutrients like fats and proteins. Instantly cooling the milk prevents the heat-sensitive nutrients from getting lost. 

HOW ANIMAL PROTECTION IS RELATED WITH HIGH-QUALITY COOLING?

High-quality cooling helps protect animal well-being by protecting them from heat stress, thus preventing overall health and productivity. Highly advanced cooling equipment also detects any residue left from the previous batch so that there is no contamination left; for this reason, this equipment is auto-cleaning to provide the most nutritious milk produced from the healthy cows.

SURVIVAL IN A STANDARDIZED MARKET

In today’s modern era, only the best is preferred, and the rest are neglected as quality matters more for all the products. Only the best quality milk stands out in the market, even if it is digital or not. Milk quality depends on the farm’s stability. Precision cooling process qualified with IoT provides a digital “cold chain” certificate. It provides the authority of the proof to the marketers about the authenticity and quality of the milk. It guarantees them that you are provided with  the best quality of milk without any alteration that has been passed through the cooling standards. By doing this, the farms secure their place in the global market.

CONCLUSION

In conclusion, precision cooling is not only a casual technique, but it has also become a necessity to perform this technique in farms to maintain quality. By stopping the bacterial growth just after the milk is extracted from  the cow, it prevents the wastage of time and efforts made to upkeep the health of the cow and the healthy feed given to them. NK Dairy equipment provides the best AI inclusive tools and equipment of modern times that will help you to know, not only how the milk is produced but also the way milk is preserved, for the further queries get in touch with our expert Mrs. Himpreet Kaur. 

FAQs

  • What is the maximum temperature we can keep milk storage at?

For maintaining all the quality standards, the suggested temperature at which the milk must be stored is four degrees Celsius.

  • Does precision cooling equipment consume excessive energy?

This technique takes the heat removed from the milk, makes hot water from it and uses that for farm sanitation, saving extra energy costs.

title.rendered: Why Is Compliance Necessary For Dairy Equipment Manufacturers In India? Read To Find Out

content.rendered:
Dairy equipment companies hold great significance in providing modern and technologically advanced dairy equipment that simplifies the task of preparing dairy products. With the comprehensive utilization of dairy processing equipment, such as khoya-making machines or curd-making machines, one can get the desirable results in terms of texture and taste. In order to maintain the brilliant services, most of the dairy equipment companies build compliance that eventually leads to consumer trust and high market success. Building compliance is necessary for the dairy equipment companies in order to maintain consumer trust by providing safe dairy equipment. Along with this, ensuring that the dairy equipment company follows the specific rules to take care of the health of consumers highlights the necessity of building compliance. To know more about the necessity of building compliance in a dairy equipment company, read this blog in an essential manner. 

Understand the necessity of building compliance in a dairy equipment company. 

Building compliance helps in securing public health in an essential manner. Understanding the necessity of building compliance is necessary to protect public health by providing them with the dairy equipment. Considering this, have a look at the points mentioned below reflecting the necessity of compliance in a dairy equipment company. 

To ensure that products prevent contamination: Usually, dairy equipment is more vulnerable to the pathogens which can affect the health of the consumers. Therefore, compliance is necessary to ensure that the dairy equipment prevents contamination, as a result of this, consumers’ health remains unaffected. Following this, compliance is necessary to safeguard the consumer’s health in an essential manner. 

To ensure that the product includes precise temperature: Building compliance in dairy equipment companies helps in ensuring that the specific dairy equipment holds precise temperature, storage and processing. This is crucial in order to prepare the milk products at an accurate temperature, hence providing better quality milk products. 

To ensure that products align with specific standards: Compliance is necessary for the dairy equipment companies in order to align their equipment with specific standards, such as ISO. This helps in providing a different and unique recognition to the specific dairy equipment companies, which is advantageous to stay in the crowded market. 

To ensure that the products match the legal requirements: For the dairy equipment companies, it is necessary to build compliance in order to align with the legal requirements, which include food safety, product quality and hygienic environment. This is necessary in order to stay preventive from legal penalties and fines in an essential manner.

Conclusion

It is necessary to build compliance in dairy equipment companies to ensure that the consumer’s health remains unaffected. Along with this, compliance is necessary to align with the legal requirements and stay protected from legal fines and penalties. The above-mentioned points vividly demonstrate the necessity of compliance in dairy equipment companies in India. Considering this, NK Dairy Equipment follows all the necessary compliances, due to which your health remains unaffected, and you can rely on us blindly to witness the amazing quality of our dairy equipment. Each process is monitored by our professional, Mrs. Himpreet Kaur, in order to provide you with the superior quality of dairy processing machines.

FAQs

Make sure that you look for the ISI mark as it indicates the quality of the dairy equipment. It is important to look for this mark as it assures you of the safety and hygiene of the dairy processing machines. 

     2.  Which documents need to be maintained in compliance with dairy equipment companies?

Documents such as test patches of milk, logs of milk collected and the maintenance of the equipment need to be maintained under compliance.

title.rendered: Have You Wondered What Oat Milk Is? Let’s Explore Its Processing!

content.rendered:
Oat milk — the best choice for people who prefer a vegan diet, as it is a plant-based alternative to milk, which is getting popular day by day. It is made from oats, which are blended with water, and therefore has a creamy texture. Oat milk is known for its slightly sweet flavour, which is often mild. Moreover, its versatility makes it an ideal choice for cereal, coffee and baking. It is a good option for those who are allergic to nuts, soy or dairy products. It is much more sustainable than dairy milk as it uses less land and water. It is usually fortified with a variety of vitamins such as B12, calcium, and Vitamin D as well. As compared to the milk of the cow, it has lower protein content.

The processing of oat milk 

The transformation of oats into a dairy-free as well as a creamy drink through the processes of milling, enzymatic treatment, separation, formulation, homogenization, and sterilization. In the process, oats are first mixed with water, starches are broken down by the enzymes, such as amylase, in order to get a smooth texture – liquefaction as well as sweetness. Thereafter, through the process of centrifugation, the liquid is separated from solids. Eventually, vitamins, oils and stabilizers are added to the milk, which is followed by the process of homogenization to achieve a smooth texture and also aseptic filling or UHT treatment for the shelf stability. 

Important steps in the production of oat milk 

  • Milling: Rolled oats or oat flour are ground, which is then mixed with water in order to form a slurry.
  • Enzymatic Hydrolysis: Also known as Liquefaction and Saccharification, this process involves the breaking down of oat starches into simpler forms of sugars, such as maltose, in which the enzymes are added. This reduces viscosity, thereby creating natural sweetness. Heat can also be used in this process.
  • Decanting: In the process of separation, the liquid oat base is separated from the insoluble fibre – oat pulp, by a centrifuge. This also maximises yield.
  • Formulation: Various ingredients such as vitamins, minerals, and stabilisers are blended in with plant oils for creaminess, texture, flavour and nutrition.
  • Homogenization: It involves the use of tiny nozzles at high pressure; the mixture is then forced to break down fat droplets, thereby creating a uniform and smooth texture, which prevents separation thereafter.
  • Pasteurisation Heat Treatment: To kill enzymes and microorganisms, the oat milk is often heated at an Ultra-High Temperature to ensure a long shelf life of the oat milk.
  • Aseptic Filling and Packaging: The oat milk, which is sterilised, is kept in sterilized cartons in a sterile environment. This helps in keeping the oat milk fresh without any need to refrigerate until it is opened.

Conclusion 

Oat milk is an eco-friendly alternative to dairy milk as it has lower water and land requirements. Moreover, it is getting popular with baristas and also home cooks for use in beverages as well as various other recipes for its creamy and smooth texture. For replacing animal-derived milk, oat milk is the best choice for you. NK Dairy Equipment provides the best and highest-quality vegan products, and the oat milk processing plant is one of them. Mrs Himpreet Kaur monitors the area and ensures cleanliness around every corner. With more than 20 years of experience, we are happy to receive an enormous number of clients. You can also be one of them. Visit us and take with you an oat milk processing plant today.
